description: This optical micrograph captured using differential interference contrast (DIC) techniques shows the microstructure of hot-rolled Fe-30Mn-9Al-1.01Si-0.9C-0.5Mo alloy in the peak aged condition (60 hours at 530°C), etched with nital. The image reveals equiaxed austenite grains with an average diameter of 10–20 μm, containing visible annealing twins within the grains. Prominent preferential grain boundary etching is observed, which caption states is due to κ-carbide precipitation on grain boundaries during aging. Banding structures resulting from alloy segregation are also visible across the field. This microstructure corresponds to the peak aged condition where grain boundary κ-carbide precipitation has occurred, which citation sentences report reduces elongation from 54% to 14% compared to the solution-treated state. The equiaxed grain structure was produced by hot rolling at 900°C to 84% reduction followed by solution treatment at 900°C for 10 minutes and water quenching. The figure supports the core conclusion that while hot rolling refines grain size and improves ductility in the solution-treated condition, peak aging causes detrimental κ-carbide precipitation at grain boundaries that significantly impairs tensile elongation.
